info.full_name: wastewater_covid
info.long_name: Wastewater Viral Activity Level of COVID-19
info.short_description: Wastewater Viral Activity Level of COVID-19.
info.long_description: Wastewater Viral Activity Level (VAL) of COVID-19. The viral activity level is the scaled number of standard deviations above a dynamic baseline. The baseline is the 10th percentile of the log-transformed and normalized concentration values, which is calculated within a 12 month timeframe every 6 months. This is then scaled, where e is the mathmatical constant, and x is the number of standard deviations: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mi>V</mi><mi>A</mi><mi>L</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">VAL = e^{x}</annotation></semantics></math> These values are categorized as very low, low, moderate, high, or very high using the following breakpoints: 1.5, 3, 4.5, and 8.
info.statement: The viral activity level of COVID-19 in wastewater is {value}.
info.unit: linear scaling of log standard deviations above a baseline

sources.name: National Wastewater Surveillance System
sources.location: Wastewater COVID-19 State and Territory Trends

info.full_name: wastewater_flua
info.long_name: Wastewater Viral Activity Level of Influenza A
info.short_description: Wastewater Viral Activity Level of Influenza A.
info.long_description: Wastewater Viral Activity Level (VAL) of Influenza A. The viral activity level is the scaled number of standard deviations above a dynamic baseline. The baseline is the 10th percentile of the log-transformed and normalized concentration values, which is calculated within a 18 month timeframe every year. This is then scaled, where e is the mathmatical constant, and x is the number of standard deviations: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mi>V</mi><mi>A</mi><mi>L</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">VAL = e^{x}</annotation></semantics></math> These values are categorized as very low, low, moderate, high, or very high using the following breakpoints: 1.6, 4.5, 12.2, and 20.1.
info.statement: The viral activity level of Influenza A in wastewater is {value}.
info.unit: linear scaling of log standard deviations above a baseline

info.short_description: Wastewater Viral Activity Level of RSV.
info.long_description: Wastewater Viral Activity Level (VAL) of RSV. The viral activity level is the scaled number of standard deviations above a dynamic baseline. The baseline is the 10th percentile of the log-transformed and normalized concentration values, which is calculated within a 18 month timeframe every year. This is then scaled, where e is the mathmatical constant, and x is the number of standard deviations: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="block"><semantics><mrow><mi>V</mi><mi>A</mi><mi>L</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>x</mi></msup></mrow><annotation encoding="application/x-tex">VAL = e^{x}</annotation></semantics></math> These values are categorized as very low, low, moderate, high, or very high using the following breakpoints: 4, 8, 12, and 20.
info.statement: The viral activity level of RSV in wastewater is {value}.
info.unit: linear scaling of log standard deviations above a baseline
